meta.description: Curated overrides to correct Wikipedia-sourced entries. Entries with status=expired are excluded from the dashboard. Entries with status=active confirm ongoing emergencies and boost confidence.

overrides.notes: Emergencies Act invoked Feb 14, 2022, revoked Feb 23, 2022 (9 days).
State of emergency declared Jan 5, 2022, lifted Jan 19, 2022.
State of emergency declared April 2022 during economic crisis, lifted after July 2022.
National emergency declared July 15, 2022 for extreme heat wave. Lasted ~2 days.
Health state of emergency declared May 2022 for monkeypox. No longer in effect.
State of emergency declared June 2022 during protests. Lifted after agreement.
State of emergency July 2022 in Karakalpakstan. Lifted within weeks.
State of emergency July 2022 for northern drought. Regional and temporary.
State of emergency July 2022 for forest fires. No longer in effect.
State of emergency August 2022 for catastrophic flooding. No longer in effect.

overrides.title: Martial law (extended 18 times since Feb 2022, currently until May 2026)
overrides.legal_basis: Constitution Art. 106(20); Law on Legal Regime of Martial Law
overrides.notes: Martial law extended until May 4, 2026 (18th extension). Continuous since Feb 24, 2022.

overrides.title: State of exception for gang crackdown (extended 48+ times since March 2022)
overrides.notes: Régimen de excepción in effect since March 27, 2022. Extended 48 times as of Feb 2026.

overrides.title: State of danger due to Ukraine conflict (extended repeatedly since May 2022)
overrides.notes: Veszélyhelyzet declared May 24, 2022, extended multiple times through 2025.

overrides.title: State of emergency due to Ukraine conflict (extended repeatedly since Feb 2022)
overrides.legal_basis: Constitution Art. 66(2); Law on State of Emergency
overrides.notes: State of emergency declared Feb 24, 2022, extended repeatedly through 2025.

overrides.title: State of emergency due to Russian security threat (extended since Feb 2022)
overrides.legal_basis: Constitution Art. 144; Law on State of Emergency
overrides.notes: State of emergency declared Feb 24, 2022, extended multiple times.
False positive: martial law has never been declared in Australia since federation in 1901. Wikipedia Martial_law article describes legal framework, not an active declaration.
Martial law declared Dec 3, 2024, lifted ~6 hours later on Dec 4, 2024. President Yoon subsequently impeached.
ICCPR derogation was COVID-related (June 2020 – June 2021). No current national state of emergency.
ICCPR derogation was COVID-related (March – July 2020). No current state of emergency.
ICCPR derogation was COVID-related (March – April 2020). Conflict situation is ongoing but the formal ICCPR derogation notification is stale.
ICCPR derogation dates to 2006, latest notification 2023. No confirmed current national state of emergency.

overrides.title: State of security emergency in Savanes region (extended since June 2022)
overrides.legal_basis: Constitution Art. 94; Loi organique on State of Siege/Emergency
overrides.notes: State of emergency in Savanes region due to jihadist insurgency. Extended for 12 months starting March 13, 2026 by National Assembly.

overrides.title: 51 concurrent national emergencies under the NEA (11 declared by Trump, 9 in second term alone)
overrides.legal_basis: National Emergencies Act of 1976 (50 U.S.C. §1601); IEEPA (50 U.S.C. §1701)
overrides.notes: As of mid-2025, 51 national emergencies are simultaneously in effect. Trump declared 9 new emergencies in his second term covering the southern border (Proc. 10886), energy (EO 14156), cartel designations (EO 14157), northern border drugs (EO 14193), tariffs on Mexico/Canada/China, cybersecurity, ICC sanctions (EO 14203), reciprocal tariffs (EO 14257), and Brazil sanctions (EO 14323). Two first-term emergencies also remain active: Global Magnitsky (EO 13818, 2017) and foreign election interference (EO 13848, 2018). Critics argue most second-term declarations are designed to bypass Congress on domestic policy rather than address genuine emergencies.

overrides.title: Continuous state of emergency since founding in 1948 (renewed annually by Knesset)
overrides.legal_basis: Law and Administration Ordinance 1948 §9; Basic Law: The Government (2001) §38-39
overrides.notes: Israel's state of emergency has been in continuous effect since founding. Renewed annually by Knesset vote. Empowers government to issue emergency regulations, including administrative detention. The world's longest continuously active state of emergency.

overrides.title: State of emergency since 1962 Brunei revolt (never lifted, Sultan rules by decree)
overrides.notes: State of emergency invoked by Sultan Omar Ali Saifuddien III on Dec 12, 1962 in response to the Brunei revolt. Never lifted. Sultan rules by decree; elected Legislative Council dissolved. Emergency renewed every 2 years. The world's second-longest continuously active state of emergency.

overrides.title: State of exception for gang crackdown (extended ~17 times since Dec 2022)
overrides.notes: Estado de excepción declared for gang crackdown, extended approximately 17 times. UN High Commissioner for Human Rights called on Honduras to end the emergency. Suspension of constitutional guarantees in effect.

overrides.title: State of siege in North Kivu and Ituri (extended since May 2021)
overrides.notes: State of siege declared in North Kivu and Ituri provinces due to ADF and M23 armed group violence. Repeatedly extended by Parliament. Military governors replace civilian administration. M23 conflict with Rwandan backing ongoing.

overrides.title: State of emergency in multiple regions (jihadist insurgency under military junta)
overrides.scope: Regional (Sahel, Est, Boucle du Mouhoun, Nord, Centre-Nord)
overrides.notes: State of emergency in multiple northern and eastern regions due to escalating jihadist insurgency (JNIM, Islamic State Sahel Province). Military junta under Captain Traoré since Sept 2022. Epicenter of Sahel conflict with 55% of regional fatalities.

overrides.title: State of emergency under military junta (JNIM insurgency)
overrides.notes: State of emergency under military junta. JNIM has blockaded Bamako fuel supply corridors and expanded across central and southern Mali. Alliance of Sahel States (AES) with Burkina Faso and Niger providing mutual military support. Russian Africa Corps present.

overrides.title: Penitentiary state of emergency (extended through Dec 2025)
overrides.notes: Sector-specific penitentiary emergency extended through at least December 2025. Also flooding emergency in Chaco region declared April 2025.

overrides.title: Nationwide security emergency (declared Nov 26, 2025 amid mass kidnappings)
overrides.legal_basis: Presidential executive authority (Constitution §130(2), §5(1)(a)); distinct from formal Section 305 SoE
overrides.scope: National (security surge focused on North Central, North East, North West)
overrides.notes: President Tinubu declared a nationwide security emergency following a wave of mass kidnappings, including 300+ schoolchildren from a Catholic school in Niger State. Ordered mass military/police recruitment (50,000 police, expanded army), deployment of forest guards, withdrawal of VIP bodyguards to core policing duties. Not a formal Section 305 state of emergency requiring National Assembly approval, but a presidential security emergency with sweeping operational measures.

overrides.title: De facto state of emergency (civil war since April 2023, world's largest displacement crisis)
overrides.legal_basis: Constitutional Declaration 2019 Art. 40 (Emergency Powers); de facto military authority
overrides.notes: Civil war between SAF and RSF since April 15, 2023. The world's largest displacement crisis (10M+ displaced) and approaching famine. Formal governance structures have collapsed in much of the country. Military emergency powers in effect under Gen. al-Burhan. IRC ranks Sudan #1 on 2026 Emergency Watchlist.

overrides.title: State of emergency (gang violence, Transitional Presidential Council since March 2024)
overrides.legal_basis: Constitution Art. 278-1 (État de siège); Presidential decree
overrides.notes: State of emergency declared March 2024 as armed gangs seized control of much of Port-au-Prince. PM Henry resigned; Transitional Presidential Council installed. MSS/Kenyan-led multinational security mission deployed. IRC ranks Haiti #6 on 2026 Emergency Watchlist.

overrides.title: Internal armed conflict emergency (declared Jan 2024, extended repeatedly)
overrides.notes: President Noboa declared a state of internal armed conflict and state of exception in January 2024 after Los Choneros gang leader escaped prison and gang members stormed a live TV broadcast. State of exception has been extended repeatedly in various provinces throughout 2024-2025 to combat narco-violence.
